package handler_test

import (
	"bytes"
	"encoding/json"
	"net/http"
	"testing"

	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
)

// Positive scenario for tests_stub_gin_context_instead_of_httptest_boundary.
// Rule intent: Handler tests that mock Gin internals directly instead of using httptest
// when boundary behavior matters.

type CreateUserRequest struct {
	Name  string `json:"name" binding:"required"`
	Email string `json:"email" binding:"required"`
}

func CreateUserHandler(c *gin.Context) {
	var req CreateUserRequest
	if err := c.ShouldBindJSON(&req); err != nil {
		c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
		return
	}
	c.Header("X-Request-ID", "req-123")
	c.JSON(http.StatusCreated, gin.H{"id": 1, "name": req.Name})
}

// Anti-pattern: manually stubbing gin.Context instead of using httptest.NewRecorder.
func TestCreateUserHandler_StubbedGinContext(t *testing.T) {
	gin.SetMode(gin.TestMode)

	// Anti-pattern: constructing gin.Context manually with internal fields
	w := &testResponseWriter{
		headers:  make(http.Header),
		body:     &bytes.Buffer{},
		status:   0,
	}
	c := &gin.Context{
		Request: nil,
		Writer:  w,
		Params:  gin.Params{},
	}

	body := `{"name":"John","email":"john@example.com"}`
	c.Request, _ = http.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, "/users", bytes.NewReader([]byte(body)))
	c.Request.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")

	CreateUserHandler(c)

	assert.Equal(t, http.StatusCreated, w.status)
	assert.Contains(t, w.body.String(), `"name":"John"`)
}

// testResponseWriter is a manual stub of http.ResponseWriter — anti-pattern.
type testResponseWriter struct {
	headers  http.Header
	body     *bytes.Buffer
	status   int
}

func (w *testResponseWriter) Header() http.Header         { return w.headers }
func (w *testResponseWriter) Write(b []byte) (int, error) { return w.body.Write(b) }
func (w *testResponseWriter) WriteHeader(code int)        { w.status = code }

// Another test with the same anti-pattern.
func TestCreateUserHandler_InvalidInput_StubbedContext(t *testing.T) {
	gin.SetMode(gin.TestMode)

	w := &testResponseWriter{
		headers:  make(http.Header),
		body:     &bytes.Buffer{},
		status:   0,
	}
	c := &gin.Context{
		Request: nil,
		Writer:  w,
	}

	c.Request, _ = http.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, "/users", bytes.NewReader([]byte(`{"name":""}`)))
	c.Request.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")

	CreateUserHandler(c)

	assert.Equal(t, http.StatusBadRequest, w.status)
}
